The companion to v4.9.1 — the second half of the #44 "orphan container" report.

## Fixed

- **The Recovery tab's readability check no longer resurrects deleted-container entries.** (#44, thanks @manilx) When you removed a container that BombVault had backed up (for example, swapping a Docker container for a plugin), a "not installed" orphan entry could reappear on the Containers page "out of nowhere" — without anyone clicking **Discover backups**. The cause: the Recovery tab's "is my backup readable?" check (Step 1 *Re-check* / Step 2 *Connect & preview*) ran `discover` purely as a probe, but `discover` has a write side-effect — it rebuilds the target list from the backups still in the repository. So merely checking readability silently re-added every container that still had a backup sitting in the repo. The readability check is now **read-only**: it opens and decrypts the repository to confirm it is reachable and your `APP_KEY` is correct, but writes nothing. Only the explicit **Discover backups** action (Containers/VMs page, or Recovery Step 3) rebuilds targets.

v4.9.1 already fixed the misleading "Last backup: Never" on these rows; to remove a leftover entry for good, use **Delete all backups** on it.

---
